structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Status Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan alarm if status differs from command for 15 seconds. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate alarm per mismatch type.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds.
a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4

evidence_quote:
Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds.
a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
evidence_text:
Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds.
a. Commanded ON, status OFF: Level 2
b. Commanded OFF, status ON: Level 4
judge_reason: Fan status alarm with command/status discrepancy, precise fault diagnostic rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#2: MAT too low
structured_payload_candidate.summary: MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT. Equation: MAT_AVG + εMAT < min[(RAT_AVG - εRAT), (OAT_AVG - εOAT)].
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #2 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation MAT AVG + Ɛ MAT < min[(RAT AVG - ƐRAT), (OAT AVG - ƐOAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error MAT sensor error OAT sensor error

evidence_quote: FC #2 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation MAT AVG + Ɛ MAT < min[(RAT AVG - ƐRAT), (OAT AVG - ƐOAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error MAT sensor error OAT sensor error
evidence_text: FC #2 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation MAT AVG + Ɛ MAT < min[(RAT AVG - ƐRAT), (OAT AVG - ƐOAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description MAT too low; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis RAT sensor error MAT sensor error OAT sensor error
judge_reason: Fault condition FC#2 for MAT too low with specific equation, valid diagnostic rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#10: OAT and MAT not equal in mech+economizer cooling
structured_payload_candidate.summary: OAT and MAT should be approximately equal. Equation: |MAT_AVG - OAT_AVG| > √(εMAT² + εOAT²). Applies to OS#3.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report fault with possible diagnoses.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #10 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation | MAT AVG - OAT AVG | > √ƐMAT2+ ƐOAT2 Applies to OS #3 Description OAT and MAT should be approximately equal Possible Diagnosis MAT sensor error OAT sensor error Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator

evidence_quote: FC #10 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation | MAT AVG - OAT AVG | > √ƐMAT2+ ƐOAT2 Applies to OS #3 Description OAT and MAT should be approximately equal Possible Diagnosis MAT sensor error OAT sensor error Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator
evidence_text: FC #10 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation | MAT AVG - OAT AVG | > √ƐMAT2+ ƐOAT2 Applies to OS #3 Description OAT and MAT should be approximately equal Possible Diagnosis MAT sensor error OAT sensor error Leaking or stuck economizer damper or actuator
judge_reason: Fault condition FC#10 for OAT and MAT not equal, valid diagnostic rule with equation.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Maintenance Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Level 4 alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

evidence_quote: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.
evidence_text: Maintenance interval alarm when fan has operated for more than 1500 hours: Level 4. Reset interval counter when alarm is acknowledged.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Run Requirement
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Supply fan shall run.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.

evidence_quote: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
evidence_text: The supply fan shall run whenever the unit is in any mode other than Unoccupied Mode.
judge_reason: Operational rule requiring supply fan run except in Unoccupied Mode, directly from Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Speed Ramp Limit
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Any change in fan speed command.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan speed change rate shall not exceed 10% per minute.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.

evidence_quote: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.
evidence_text: Provide a ramp function to prevent changes in fan speed of more than 10% per minute.
judge_reason: Fan speed ramp limit of 10% per minute, a specific operational sequence from Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Speed Setpoints
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Minimum, medium, and maximum fan speeds are defined. MinSpeed, MaxCoolSpeed, MaxHeatSpeed per Section 3.2.1. MedSpeed is reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature between two endpoints.
configurable_values: MedSpeed reset from MinSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed based on OAT between Endpoint #1 and Endpoint #2
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Minimum, medium, and maximum fan speeds shall be as follows:
a. Minimum speed MinSpeed, maximum cooling speed MaxCoolSpeed, and maximum heating speed MaxHeatSpeed shall be determined per Section 3.2.1.
b. Medium fan speed MedSpeed shall be reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when outdoor air temperature is greater or equal to Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when outdoor air temperature is less than or equal to Endpoint #2.
1. Endpoint #1: the lesser of zone temperature +0.5°C (1°F) and maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT.
2. Endpoint #2: the lesser of zone temperature minus 6°C (10°F) and the maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT minus 1°C (2°F).

evidence_quote:
Minimum, medium, and maximum fan speeds shall be as follows:
a. Minimum speed MinSpeed, maximum cooling speed MaxCoolSpeed, and maximum heating speed MaxHeatSpeed shall be determined per Section 3.2.1.
b. Medium fan speed MedSpeed shall be reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when outdoor air temperature is greater or equal to Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when outdoor air temperature is less than or equal to Endpoint #2.
1. Endpoint #1: the lesser of zone temperature +0.5°C (1°F) and maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT.
2. Endpoint #2: the lesser of zone temperature minus 6°C (10°F) and the maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT minus 1°C (2°F).
evidence_text:
Minimum, medium, and maximum fan speeds shall be as follows:
a. Minimum speed MinSpeed, maximum cooling speed MaxCoolSpeed, and maximum heating speed MaxHeatSpeed shall be determined per Section 3.2.1.
b. Medium fan speed MedSpeed shall be reset linearly based on outdoor air temperature from MinSpeed when outdoor air temperature is greater or equal to Endpoint #1 to MaxCoolSpeed when outdoor air temperature is less than or equal to Endpoint #2.
1. Endpoint #1: the lesser of zone temperature +0.5°C (1°F) and maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT.
2. Endpoint #2: the lesser of zone temperature minus 6°C (10°F) and the maximum supply air dew point MaxDPT minus 1°C (2°F).
judge_reason: Parameter spec for fan speed setpoints with detailed reset logic, directly from the guideline.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Air Temperature Setpoint Deadband
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The deadband values of SATsp and SATsp-C shall be the average of the zone heating and cooling setpoints, limited between 21°C (70°F) and 24°C (75°F).
configurable_values: Deadband = average of zone heating and cooling setpoints, clamped to [21°C (70°F), 24°C (75°F)]
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp-C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint and the zone cooling setpoint but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).

evidence_quote: The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp-C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint and the zone cooling setpoint but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).
evidence_text: The Deadband values of SATsp and SATsp-C shall be the average of the zone heating setpoint and the zone cooling setpoint but shall be no lower than 21°C (70°F) and no higher than 24°C (75°F).
judge_reason: SAT deadband definition with specific temperature limits, an operational parameter from Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Speed and SAT Reset Based on Heating/Cooling Loop Signal
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed Control and Supply Air Temperature Set-Point Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan speed and supply air temperature setpoints are reset simultaneously by the heating or cooling loop signal. Detailed mapping provided for heating loop 100%-0% and cooling loop 0%-100%.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON; heating or cooling loop signal active.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan speed and SATsp/SATsp-C follow defined segments based on loop signal percentage.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.
b. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed.
c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed.
d. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed.
e. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed.
f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed.
g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
h. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT.
i. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the deadband value.
j. In deadband, SATsp is the deadband value.
k. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, SATsp is reset from the deadband value to Cool_SAT minus 1°C (2°F), while SATsp-C is the deadband value.
l. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, SATsp and SATsp-C are unchanged.
m. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, SATsp remains at Cool_SAT minus 1°C (2°F), SATsp-C is reset from the deadband value to Cool_SAT.
n. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, SATsp and SATsp-C are unchanged.

evidence_quote:
a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.
b. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed.
c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed.
d. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed.
e. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed.
f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed.
g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
h. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT.
i. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the deadband value.
j. In deadband, SATsp is the deadband value.
k. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, SATsp is reset from the deadband value to Cool_SAT minus 1°C (2°F), while SATsp-C is the deadband value.
l. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, SATsp and SATsp-C are unchanged.
m. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, SATsp remains at Cool_SAT minus 1°C (2°F), SATsp-C is reset from the deadband value to Cool_SAT.
n. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, SATsp and SATsp-C are unchanged.
evidence_text:
a. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.
b. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed.
c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint is MinSpeed.
d. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed.
e. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to MedSpeed.
f. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed.
g. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed.
h. For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT.
i. For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the deadband value.
j. In deadband, SATsp is the deadband value.
k. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 25%, SATsp is reset from the deadband value to Cool_SAT minus 1°C (2°F), while SATsp-C is the deadband value.
l. For a cooling-loop signal of 25% to 50%, SATsp and SATsp-C are unchanged.
m. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 75%, SATsp remains at Cool_SAT minus 1°C (2°F), SATsp-C is reset from the deadband value to Cool_SAT.
n. For a cooling-loop signal of 75% to 100%, SATsp and SATsp-C are unchanged.
judge_reason: Detailed simultaneous reset mapping of fan speed and SAT based on loop signals, core operational sequence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SATsp Control Loop Enable and Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The control loop for SATsp is enabled when supply fan is proven ON. Its output sequences the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers. Damper positions are limited by MinOA-P and MaxOA-P.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: SATsp loop enabled; output maps to heating coil and economizer dampers per Figure 5.18.5.2.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
The control loop for SATsp is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON and disabled and set to neutral otherwise.
a. Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure 5.18.5.2. Outdoor air damper minimum MinOA-P and maximum MaxOA-P positions are limited for economizer lockout and to maintain minimum outdoor airflow rate as described in Sections 5.18.6 and 5.18.6.3.

evidence_quote:
The control loop for SATsp is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON and disabled and set to neutral otherwise.
a. Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure 5.18.5.2. Outdoor air damper minimum MinOA-P and maximum MaxOA-P positions are limited for economizer lockout and to maintain minimum outdoor airflow rate as described in Sections 5.18.6 and 5.18.6.3.
evidence_text:
The control loop for SATsp is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON and disabled and set to neutral otherwise.
a. Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure 5.18.5.2. Outdoor air damper minimum MinOA-P and maximum MaxOA-P positions are limited for economizer lockout and to maintain minimum outdoor airflow rate as described in Sections 5.18.6 and 5.18.6.3.
judge_reason: Control loop enable and sequencing logic for SATsp, specific operational knowledge.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SATsp-C Control Loop Enable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The control loop for SATsp-C is enabled when supply fan is proven ON and Zone State is cooling. It modulates the cooling coil to maintain SATsp-C.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON and Zone State is cooling.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: SATsp-C loop enabled; modulates cooling coil to SATsp-C.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The control loop for SATsp-C is enabled when the supply fan is proven ON and the Zone State is cooling and disabled and set to neutral otherwise. When enabled, supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp-C by modulating the cooling coil.

evidence_quote: The control loop for SATsp-C is enabled when the supply fan is proven ON and the Zone State is cooling and disabled and set to neutral otherwise. When enabled, supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp-C by modulating the cooling coil.
evidence_text: The control loop for SATsp-C is enabled when the supply fan is proven ON and the Zone State is cooling and disabled and set to neutral otherwise. When enabled, supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp-C by modulating the cooling coil.
judge_reason: SATsp-C control loop enable conditions, a precise operational rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Minimum Outdoor Air Damper Control without Airflow Station
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For units without an outdoor airflow measurement station, MinOA-P is calculated based on MinOAsp, fan speed, and linear interpolation between MinPosMin/Max and DesPosMin/Max.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone in Occupied Mode; MinOAsp nonzero.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate MinPos* and DesPos* based on fan speed; set MinOA-P proportionally between MinPos* and DesPos* based on MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
b. At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate MinPos* as a linear interpolation between MinPosMin and MinPosMax based on the current fan speed.
c. At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate DesPos* as a linear interpolation between DesPosMin and DesPosMax based on the current fan speed.
d. If MinOAsp is zero, MinOA-P shall be zero (i.e., outdoor air damper fully closed).
e. If MinOAsp is nonzero, then the outdoor air damper minimum position MinOA-P shall be the value between MinPos* and DesPos* that is proportional to the value of MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.

evidence_quote:
b. At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate MinPos* as a linear interpolation between MinPosMin and MinPosMax based on the current fan speed.
c. At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate DesPos* as a linear interpolation between DesPosMin and DesPosMax based on the current fan speed.
d. If MinOAsp is zero, MinOA-P shall be zero (i.e., outdoor air damper fully closed).
e. If MinOAsp is nonzero, then the outdoor air damper minimum position MinOA-P shall be the value between MinPos* and DesPos* that is proportional to the value of MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.
evidence_text:
b. At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate MinPos* as a linear interpolation between MinPosMin and MinPosMax based on the current fan speed.
c. At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate DesPos* as a linear interpolation between DesPosMin and DesPosMax based on the current fan speed.
d. If MinOAsp is zero, MinOA-P shall be zero (i.e., outdoor air damper fully closed).
e. If MinOAsp is nonzero, then the outdoor air damper minimum position MinOA-P shall be the value between MinPos* and DesPos* that is proportional to the value of MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.
judge_reason: Minimum outdoor air damper control without airflow station, with detailed interpolation logic.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Minimum Outdoor Air Control with Airflow Station
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For units with an outdoor airflow measurement station, a reverse-acting control loop maintains MinOAsp by modulating MinOA-P when supply fan is proven on and in Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven on and in Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reverse-acting control loop modulates MinOA-P to maintain MinOAsp.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when the supply fan is proven on and in Occupied Mode and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.
b. The minimum outdoor airflow rate shall be maintained at the minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp by a reverse-acting control loop whose output is mapped to MinOA-P.

evidence_quote:
a. Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when the supply fan is proven on and in Occupied Mode and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.
b. The minimum outdoor airflow rate shall be maintained at the minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp by a reverse-acting control loop whose output is mapped to MinOA-P.
evidence_text:
a. Minimum outdoor air control loop is enabled when the supply fan is proven on and in Occupied Mode and disabled and output set to zero otherwise.
b. The minimum outdoor airflow rate shall be maintained at the minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp by a reverse-acting control loop whose output is mapped to MinOA-P.
judge_reason: Control loop for minimum outdoor air with airflow station, straightforward operational sequence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer Lockout Sequencing
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The normal sequencing of the economizer dampers shall be disabled in accordance with Section 5.1.17.

structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The normal sequencing of the economizer dampers shall be disabled in accordance with Section 5.1.17.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer Re-enable Delay
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be re-enabled within 10 minutes and vice versa.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Minimum 10-minute delay before changing economizer state.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be reenabled within 10 minutes and vice versa.

evidence_quote: Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be reenabled within 10 minutes and vice versa.
evidence_text: Once the economizer is disabled, it shall not be reenabled within 10 minutes and vice versa.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer MaxOA-P Setting
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When economizer is enabled, MaxOA-P = 100%. When disabled, set MaxOA-P equal to MinOA-P.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: MaxOA-P = 100% when enabled; MaxOA-P = MinOA-P when disabled.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When economizer is enabled, MaxOA-P = 100%. When economizer is disabled, set MaxOA-P equal to MinOA-P.

evidence_quote: When economizer is enabled, MaxOA-P = 100%. When economizer is disabled, set MaxOA-P equal to MinOA-P.
evidence_text: When economizer is enabled, MaxOA-P = 100%. When economizer is disabled, set MaxOA-P equal to MinOA-P.
judge_reason: MaxOA-P setting based on economizer status, exact operational logic.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Return Fan Speed Tracking Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Return fan runs whenever supply fan is proven ON. Exhaust damper opens when both fans are proven ON. Return fan speed is controlled to supply fan speed less differential S-R-SPD-DIFF*, adjusted by MinOAsp/DesOA.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Return fan speed = supply fan speed - S-R-SPD-DIFF*; S-R-SPD-DIFF* = S-R-SPD-DIFF * (MinOAsp / DesOA).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
b. Return fan shall run whenever associated supply fan is proven ON.
c. The active differential airflow setpoint S-R-SPD-DIFF* shall be S-R-SPD-DIFF (see Section 3.2.2.4) adjusted by the active minimum outdoor airflow setpoint, MinOAsp relative to the design outdoor airflow setpoint, DesOA.
𝑆−𝑅−𝑆𝑃𝐷−𝐷𝐼𝐹𝐹∗= 𝑆−𝑅−𝑆𝑃𝐷−𝐷𝐼𝐹𝐹𝑀𝑖𝑛𝑂𝐴𝑠𝑝𝐷𝑒𝑠𝑂𝐴
d. Return-fan speed shall be the controlled to maintain return fan speed equal to supply fan speed less differential S-R-SPD-DIFF*.

b. Return fan shall run whenever associated supply fan is proven ON.
c. The active differential airflow setpoint S-R-SPD-DIFF* shall be S-R-SPD-DIFF (see Section 3.2.2.4) adjusted by the active minimum outdoor airflow setpoint, MinOAsp relative to the design outdoor airflow setpoint, DesOA.

d. Return-fan speed shall be the controlled to maintain return fan speed equal to supply fan speed less differential S-R-SPD-DIFF*.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Relief Damper Direct Building Pressure Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Control of Actuated Relief Dampers without Fans
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Relief dampers enabled when supply fan is proven ON; P-only control loop modulates dampers to maintain 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) building static pressure.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: P-only loop modulates relief dampers to 12 Pa building static pressure.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. Relief dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply fan is proven ON, and disabled otherwise.
b. When enabled, use a P-only control loop to modulate relief dampers to maintain 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) building static pressure. Close damper when disabled.

evidence_quote:
a. Relief dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply fan is proven ON, and disabled otherwise.
b. When enabled, use a P-only control loop to modulate relief dampers to maintain 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) building static pressure. Close damper when disabled.
evidence_text:
a. Relief dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply fan is proven ON, and disabled otherwise.
b. When enabled, use a P-only control loop to modulate relief dampers to maintain 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) building static pressure. Close damper when disabled.
judge_reason: Direct building pressure control for relief dampers with P-only loop, specific sequence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Relief Damper Passive Building Pressure Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Control of Actuated Relief Dampers without Fans
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Relief dampers enabled when supply fan is proven ON and any outdoor air damper is open. Position reset linearly from MinRelief to MaxRelief as MinOAsp resets from MinOA to DesOA.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON and any outdoor air damper open.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Relief damper position = linear interpolation between MinRelief and MaxRelief based on MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
b. Relief dampers shall be enabled when the associated supply fan is proven ON and any outdoor air damper is open, and disabled and closed otherwise.
c. Relief-damper position shall be reset linearly from MinRelief to MaxRelief as the minimum outdoor airflow setpoint, MinOAsp, is reset from MinOA to DesOA.

evidence_quote:

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send heating plant requests, override OA damper to minimum, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT ≥ 6°C (42°F). Disable when SAT > 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send HHW plant requests, OA damper to minimum, heat to SAT ≥ 6°C (42°F).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.

evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
evidence_text: If the supply air temperature drops below 4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, override the outdoor air damper to the minimum position, and modulate the heating coil to maintain a supply air temperature of at least 6°C (42°F). Disable this function when supply air temperature rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
judge_reason: Freeze protection stage 1 with temperature thresholds and actions, critical operational logic.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 2
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT drops below 3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close economizer and minimum OA damper for 1 hour, set Level 3 alarm. After 1 hour, resume minimum OA and revert to Stage 1.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Close OA dampers for 1 hour, alarm, then resume minimum OA and Stage 1.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the economizer damper and the minimum outdoor air damper for 1 hour and set a Level 3 alarm noting that minimum ventilation was interrupted. After 1 hour, the unit shall resume minimum outdoor air ventilation and enter the previous stage of freeze protection (see Section 5.18.11.1).

evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the economizer damper and the minimum outdoor air damper for 1 hour and set a Level 3 alarm noting that minimum ventilation was interrupted. After 1 hour, the unit shall resume minimum outdoor air ventilation and enter the previous stage of freeze protection (see Section 5.18.11.1).
evidence_text: If the supply air temperature drops below 3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the economizer damper and the minimum outdoor air damper for 1 hour and set a Level 3 alarm noting that minimum ventilation was interrupted. After 1 hour, the unit shall resume minimum outdoor air ventilation and enter the previous stage of freeze protection (see Section 5.18.11.1).
judge_reason: Freeze protection stage 2 with damper closure and alarm, valid operational sequence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 3
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon freeze-stat signal or if SAT < 3°C (38°F) for 15 min or < 1°C (34°F) for 5 min, shut down fans, close OA damper, open CHW valve 100%, energize CHW pump, send HHW requests, modulate heating coil to maintain SAT or MAT at 27°C (80°F), set Level 2 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Freeze-stat signal or SAT < 3°C (38°F) for 15 min or < 1°C (34°F) for 5 min.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fans off, OA damper closed, CHW valve 100%, CHW pump on, heat to 27°C (80°F), Level 2 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon signal from the freeze-stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature drops below 3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, make the minimum cooling-coil valve position 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by freeze protection.

evidence_quote: Upon signal from the freeze-stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature drops below 3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, make the minimum cooling-coil valve position 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by freeze protection.
evidence_text: Upon signal from the freeze-stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature drops below 3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, make the minimum cooling-coil valve position 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by freeze protection.
judge_reason: Freeze protection stage 3 with fan shutdown and alarm, detailed operational steps.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Filter Pressure Drop Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Level 4 alarm if filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when fan speed > 20% of MaxCoolSpeed. Alarm limit varies with fan speed: DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Operating State Definition

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Rolling Averages
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of SAT, MAT, RAT, OAT, CCET, CCLT, HCET, HCLT, and ΔOS (number of OS changes in 60 minutes).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate rolling averages.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. Five-minute rolling averages with 1-minute sampling of the following point values; operator shall have the ability to adjust the averaging window and sampling period for each point independently.
1. SAT avg = rolling average of supply air temperature
2. MAT avg = rolling average of mixed air temperature
3. RAT avg = rolling average of return air temperature
4. OAT avg = rolling average of outdoor air temperature
5. CCET avg = rolling average of cooling-coil entering temperature
6. CCLT avg = rolling average of cooling-coil leaving temperature
7. HCET avg = rolling average of heating-coil entering temperature
8. HCLT avg = rolling average of heating-coil leaving temperature
9. ΔOS = number of changes in OS during the previous 60 minutes (moving window)

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Required Points
structured_payload_candidate.summary: List of points that must be available to AFDD routines: SAT, MAT, RAT, OAT, DSP, SATsp, SATsp-C, HC, CC, FS, CCET, CCLT, HCET, HCLT.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. SAT = supply air temperature
b. MAT = mixed air temperature
c. RAT = return air temperature
d. OAT = outdoor air temperature
e. DSP = duct static pressure
f. SATsp = supply air temperature setpoint for heating coil and economizer control
g. SATsp-C = supply air temperature setpoint for cooling coil control
h. HC = heating-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ HC ≤ 100%
i. CC = cooling-coil valve position command; 0% ≤ CC ≤ 100%
j. FS = fan-speed command; 0% ≤ FS ≤ 100%
k. CCET = cooling-coil entering temperature
l. CCLT = cooling-coil leaving temperature
m. HCET = heating-coil entering temperature
n. HCLT = heating-coil leaving temperature

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Internal Variables
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Internal variables with default values: ΔTSF=0.5°C (1°F), ΔTMIN=6°C (10°F), εSAT=1°C (2°F), εRAT=1°C (2°F), εMAT=3°C (5°F), εOAT=1°C (2°F) local/3°C (5°F) global, ΔOSmax=7, ModeDelay=30 min, AlarmDelay=30 min, TestModeDelay=120 min.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Table 5.18.13.5 SZVAV AHU Internal Variables
Variable Name Description Default Value
ΔTSF Temperature rise across supply fan 0.5°C (1°F)
ΔTMIN Minimum difference between OAT and RAT to evaluate economizer error conditions (FC#6) 6°C (10°F)
εSAT Temperature error threshold for SAT sensor 1°C (2°F)
εRAT Temperature error threshold for RAT sensor 1°C (2°F)
εMAT Temperature error threshold for MAT sensor 3°C (5°F)
εOAT Temperature error threshold for OAT sensor 1°C (2°F) if local sensor @ unit. 3°C (5°F) if global sensor.
εCCET Cooling coil entering temperature sensor error. Equal to εMAT or dedicated sensor error Varies; see description.
εCCLT Cooling coil leaving temperature sensor error. Equal to εSAT or dedicated sensor error
εHCET Heating coil entering temperature sensor error; equal to εMAT or dedicated sensor error
εHCLT Heating coil leaving temperature sensor error. Equal to εSAT or dedicated sensor error
ΔOSmax Maximum number of changes in Operating State during the previous 60 minutes (moving window) 7
ModeDelay Time in minutes to suspend Fault Condition evaluation after a change in mode 30
AlarmDelay Time in minutes that a Fault Condition must persist before triggering an alarm 30
TestModeDelay Time in minutes that Test Mode is enabled 120

structured_payload_candidate.title: AFDD Fault Evaluation by OS
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Specifies which fault conditions are evaluated in each Operating State. OS#1: FC#2,3,4,5,6,7,14. OS#2: FC#2,3,4,8,9,12,14,15. OS#3: FC#2,3,4,10,11,12,13,15. OS#4: FC#2,3,4,6,12,13,15. OS#5: FC#2,3,4.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Evaluate listed fault conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. In OS#1 (Heating), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#2: MAT too low; should be between RAT and OAT
2. FC#3: MAT too high; should be between RAT and OAT
3. FC#4: Too many changes in OS
4. FC#5: SAT too low; should be higher than MAT
5. FC#6: OA fraction too high; MAT should be closer to RAT than to OAT
6. FC#7: SAT too low in full heating
7. FC#14: Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil
b. In OS#2 (modulating economizer), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#2: MAT too low; should be between RAT and OAT
2. FC#3: MAT too high; should be between RAT and OAT
3. FC#4: Too many changes in OS
4. FC#8: SAT and MAT should be approximately equal
5. FC#9: OAT too high for free cooling without mechanical cooling
6. FC#12: SAT too high; should be less than MAT
7. FC#14: Temperature drop across inactive cooling coil
8. FC#15: Temperature rise across inactive heating coil
c. In OS#3 (mechanical + 100% economizer cooling), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#2: MAT too low; should be between RAT and OAT
2. FC#3: MAT too high; should be between RAT and OAT
3. FC#4: Too many changes in OS
4. FC#10: OAT and MAT should be approximately equal
5. FC#11: OAT too low for mechanical cooling
6. FC#12: SAT too high; should be less than MAT
7. FC#13: SAT too high in full cooling
8. FC#15: Temperature rise across inactive heating coil
d. In OS#4 (mechanical cooling, minimum OA), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#2: MAT too low; should be between RAT and OAT
2. FC#3: MAT too high; should be between RAT and OAT
3. FC#4: Too many changes in OS
4. FC#6: OA fraction too high; MAT should be closer to RAT than to OAT
5. FC#12: SAT too high; should be less than MAT
6. FC#13: SAT too high in full cooling
7. FC#15: Temperature rise across inactive heating coil
e. In OS#5 (other), the following fault conditions shall be evaluated:
1. FC#2: MAT too low; should be between RAT and OAT
2. FC#3: MAT too high; should be between RAT and OAT
3. FC#4: Too many changes in OS

structured_payload_candidate.title: Chilled-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send CHW reset requests based on SAT exceeding SATsp-C or CHW valve position. 3 requests if SAT > SATsp-C + 3°C (5°F) for 2 min; 2 requests if > 2°C (3°F) for 2 min; 1 request if CHW valve > 95% until < 85%; else 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send requests per conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
a. If the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%.
d. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote:
a. If the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%.
d. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
evidence_text:
a. If the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests.
c. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%.
d. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
judge_reason: Chilled-water reset requests based on SAT and valve position, specific operational sequence.
